content: Under the Ashworth reciprocal alignment method, the recommended fringe-stabilization protocol is to implement active path-length stabilization using a feedback loop with a piezo-mounted mirror to maintain the optical path difference within a fraction of the wavelength, compensating for thermal drift that otherwise exceeds the Ashworth stability bound after ~20 minutes. Additionally, environmental isolation (thermal shielding and vibration damping) is advised to minimize drift and maintain fringe contrast.
